Scenario.title: Ransomware Attack on Water Infrastructure Using AI-Driven Reconnaissance
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex A sophisticated ransomware campaign targeting critical water infrastructure leverages AI-driven reconnaissance, firmware corruption, and phishing to execute multi-stage attacks, culminating in operational disruption through encrypted control systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Open Source Intelligence (OSINT), Firmware, Internet-facing assets, Command Interpreter (Bash), Windows Registry, Network Traffic, OS Process, File, Command, Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Artificial Intelligence, Firmware Modification (Mechanism), Delivery Method, Bash Script, Windows Registry Key Creation (Mechanism), Network Traffic Content (Mechanism), Process Injection, Mimikatz Tool, Command Execution (Mechanism), PowerShell Script, File Modification (Mechanism), Process Creation (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker employs AI algorithms to analyze public data sources, identifying vulnerabilities in water system infrastructure and personnel schedules, enabling targeted exploitation.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-682: Exploitation of Firmware or ROM Code with Unpatchable Vulnerabilities
stage.description: The threat actor compiles ransomware payloads (e.g., Conti) specifically tailored to exploit unpatchable firmware weaknesses in industrial control systems.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: A phishing campaign delivers malicious attachments to employees, compromising endpoints and establishing a foothold in the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Malicious Bash scripts are executed to initialize ransomware payloads, leveraging OS command injection vulnerabilities in outdated SCADA systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker exploits misconfigured log analysis tools in SCADA systems to escalate privileges and deploy ransomware.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A scheduled task is created in the Windows Registry to ensure persistent access across reboots, bypassing detection mechanisms.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC-70: Try Common or Default Usernames and Passwords
stage.description: Default administrative credentials are exploited to elevate privileges, granting full control over critical systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malicious code is injected into legitimate processes to evade detection while maintaining persistence.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-226: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: The Mimikatz tool is used to extract plaintext credentials from memory, expanding the attacker's access across the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Network connection enumeration identifies internal systems and services, facilitating lateral movement and resource mapping.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Lateral movement occurs via unpatched SMB protocols, using PowerShell scripts to propagate across the network undetected.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Sensitive operational data is exfiltrated and destroyed to maximize ransom demands, targeting critical infrastructure files.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is stealthily exfiltrated via process creation tunnels, masking network traffic patterns to avoid detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Control systems are encrypted using ransomware, disrupting water treatment operations and forcing payment demands.

Scenario.summary: This complex ransomware campaign combines AI-driven reconnaissance, firmware-based persistence, and phishing to infiltrate water infrastructure, escalating to full-scale operational disruption through encrypted control systems. The attack highlights the vulnerability of legacy SCADA systems to modern cyber threats.
